title: pouteria cinerea
intro: Pouteria cinerea, also known as the white guanabano or white soursop, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain a variety of bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and may also have anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Pouteria cinerea leaves can be consumed as a tea or used as a tincture, and the plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential use in the development of new pharmaceuticals.
